PETITION FOR INSTRUCTIONS
Off Calendar May-19-2015 Off Calendar

Petition For Removal Of Trustee Of The Greta Winter Survivor'S Trust Under The B. And G. Winter Declaration Of Trust, Petition For Removal Of Co-Trustee Of The Marital Trust Created Under The B. And G. Winter Declaration Of Trust
Off Calendar May-19-2015 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: The following matters were heard together: PTR-12-296281 The Greta Winter Survivor's Trust and PTR-13-297177 Greta Winter Irrevocable Life Insurance Trust.

Appearances: Andrew Zabronsky for (Trustee) Jack Chu. Russen Rowen for and with (Trustee/Beneficiary) Mordecai Winter. Frank Cialone for (Beneficiaries) Carin Salter & Jennifer Segal.

The Court stated that there are petitions in the 2 matters, there are 2 separate trusts, that there is a settlement agreement in the ILIT which would handle all of the matters, that we have a fully executed settlement agreement, that the only issue is whether a Guardian ad Litem is necessary and that the court does not think so. No objections were made by counsel regarding the Guardian ad Litem. The Court granted the petition for approval of settlement agreement (case 297177) (subject to review of the order). All other petitions in both cases are off calendar.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Allen. (Not Reported)

Petition For Removal Of Trustee Of The Greta Winter Survivor'S Trust Under The B. And G. Winter Declaration Of Trust, Petition For Removal Of Co-Trustee Of The Marital Trust Created Under The B. And G. Winter Declaration Of Trust
Off Calendar Jan-13-2015 Continued To Feb-03-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: The following matters were heard: PTR-12-296281 The Greta Winter Sirvivor's Trust and PTR-13-297177 The Greta Winter Irrevocable Life Insurance Trust.

Appearances: Michael Low for Schirley Zisman. Frank Cialone for Carin Salter and Jennifer Segal. Philip Welch for Mordechai Winter.

Mr. Welch stated that they continue to be extremely close to resolution, that there has been a high degree of frustration with wrapping things up, that there is yet another settlement agreement drafted, that he thinks they are now at ten drafts, that Counsel met and corresponded regularly, and that he hopes a mandatory settlement conference might bring the Parties around to getting this done. The Court informed Counsel that it can not mandate a settlement conference and that if all Parties want to take that course the Court can facilitate it through Court hospice. The Court also noted that one party was not present. Mr. Low stated that all three Parties are here on the Survivor's Trust and the Mr. Zabronsky is in the Insurance Trust ("ILIT Trust"). Both petitions for instructions, the accounting petition, the removal petition and the petition to compel, etc. were continued to February 3, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of the petitions. The Court informed Counsel that if the matter has not settled and they have not agreed to a settlement process, the matter will be set for trial. The Court also asked all Parties to step out of the courtroom to discuss when all Counsel would be available for scheduling of the settlement conference. Upon returning, the Parties indicated to the Court that all Counsel including Mr. Zabronsky will be available on Friday. The Clerk will inform all Counsel as to the Settlement Officer's availability.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy S. Park. (Not Reported)

Petition For Removal Of Trustee Of The Greta Winter Survivor'S Trust Under The B. And G. Winter Declaration Of Trust, Petition For Removal Of Co-Trustee Of The Marital Trust Created Under The B. And G. Winter Declaration Of Trust
Off Calendar Dec-09-2014 Continued To Jan-13-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: The following matters were heard: PTR-12-296281 The Greta Winter Survivor's Trust and PTR-13-297177 The Greta Winter Irrevocable Life Insurance Trust.

Appearances: Michael Low for Schirley Zisman. Thomas Latham for Jack Chu (Trustee of the Life Insurance Trust). Philip Welch for Mordechai Winter. Frank Cialone for Carin Salter and Jennifer Segal (Beneficiaries).

With respect to the Survivor's Trust Counsel reported that they are close to resolution, that have drafted a settlement agreement, that they are down to one issue, that it is a phased agreement, that there are a lot of tax issues/implications, that they expect to have it signed today, that they are going to resolve the matter within the next three weeks, that they are trying to keep costs down in the Life Insurance Trust and that is should fall in place. All matters were continued to January 13, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. McConico. (Not Reported)

Off Calendar Nov-04-2013 Continued To Dec-16-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Removal Of Trustee Of The Greta Winter Survivor'S Trust Under The B. And G. Winter Declaration Of Trust, Petition For Removal Of Co-Trustee Of The Marital Trust Created Under The B. And G. Winter Declaration Of Trust
Off Calendar Nov-04-2013 Continued To Dec-16-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Philip Welch for and with Mordechai Winter. Michael Low for and with Shirley Zisman.

Mr. Low stated that they do not have an agreement, that he requested to set for trial and that discovery is not completed. Mr. Welch requested to have a neutral trustee appointed. Counsel presented oral argument in support of their positions with regard to basis for an evidentiary hearing, sale of property, distribution of assets, removal of trustee and accounting. The Court indicated that those issues were not raised in the Examiner's notes and that it is not prepared to rule on the question of an evidentiary hearing and the issues raised. Mr. Welch requested for distribution of six assets and cash assets and that attorney's fees be approved prior to payment. Mr. Low stipulated to distribution of assets in kind pro-rata, not including fractional interest in entity. All three petitions are continued to December 16, 2013 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Mr. Low stated that issues were not property notice and interest party is not in court. Mr. Welch stated that he has no problem with noticing that party.

Judge: Paul H. Alvarado. Court Reporter: Trina Ayala Cordini CSR #12964 Phone: 415-948-8289. Clerk: Ella Yip. Bailiff: Deputy T. Bradley.

Off Calendar Sep-23-2013 Continued To Nov-04-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Michael Low for and with Schirley Zisman. Russell Rowen and Philip Welch for and with Mordechai Winter.

Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to the accounting, whether the property should be distributed pro rata or sold and distributed in cash, costs of sale of the property versus savings in pro rata distribution, what is in the best interest of the Trust, whether there is an advantage in having an evidentiary hearing, whether discovery is relevant to the issue of distribution of the property, etc. All three petitions (two Petitions for Instructions/Petition for Removal of Trustee) were continued to November 4, 2013 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status on the petitions and for filing of a petition for approval of the accounting with the expectation that the accounting petition is on file by then. The Parties are to meet and confer with regard to all other issues, with regard to what needs to happen procedurally to move this case forward and whether the issue of removal will need an evidentiary hearing. The Court expects that the issue of distribution of the property could be appropriately determined without any need of delay or expense of an evidentiary hearing. The Court also suggested that if any discovery is needed, that it be done.

Judge: Peter Busch. Court Reporter: Trina Ayala Cordini, CSR#12964, (650) 267-1302.
Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy T. Bradley. (Reported)

Petition For Instructions Pursuant To Probate Code Sec 17200
Off Calendar Sep-23-2013 Continued To Nov-04-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Removal Of Trustee Of The Greta Winter Survivor'S Trust Under The B. And G. Winter Declaration Of Trust, Petition For Removal Of Co-Trustee Of The Marital Trust Created Under The B. And G. Winter Declaration Of Trust
Off Calendar Sep-23-2013 Continued To Nov-04-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR INSTRUCTIONS
Off Calendar Jul-02-2013 Continued To Sep-23-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Removal Of Trustee Of The Greta Winter Survivor'S Trust Under The B. And G. Winter Declaration Of Trust, Petition For Removal Of Co-Trustee Of The Marital Trust Created Under The B. And G. Winter Declaration Of Trust
Off Calendar Aug-26-2013 Continued To Sep-23-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Instructions Pursuant To Probate Code Sec 17200
Off Calendar Aug-26-2013 Continued To Sep-23-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Removal Of Trustee Of The Greta Winter Survivor'S Trust Under The B. And G. Winter Declaration Of Trust, Petition For Removal Of Co-Trustee Of The Marital Trust Created Under The B. And G. Winter Declaration Of Trust
Off Calendar Jun-10-2013 Continued To Aug-26-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Michael Low for and with Shirley Zisman. Peter Welch and Russell Rowen for and with Mordechai Winters. Andrew Zabronsky for Jack Chu (interested Parties).

The Court noted that Mr. Zabronsky does not have any standing in this case, only as a creditor. Discussions were held in regards to the removal of the co-trustee of the marital trust, and the release clause. The Court agreed that the release clause in this trust is too broad and is not enforceable. Mr. Welch is to prepare a form of order. The Petition for removal of Trustee of the Greta Winter Survivor's Trust, etc. and Petition for Instructions were continued to August 26,2013 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status on discovery.

Judge: Lillian K. Sing. Court Reporter: Trina Cordini ( CSR #: 12964. Telephone #: 415-948-8289). Clerk: Nancy Bautista. Bailiff: Deputy M. Benton.

Petition For Instructions Pursuant To Probate Code Sec 17200
Off Calendar Jun-10-2013 Continued To Aug-26-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Removal Of Trustee Of The Greta Winter Survivor'S Trust Under The B. And G. Winter Declaration Of Trust, Petition For Removal Of Co-Trustee Of The Marital Trust Created Under The B. And G. Winter Declaration Of Trust
Off Calendar May-06-2013 Continued To Jun-10-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Michelle Trausch and Laila Lourdas for and with Mordechai Winter. Andrew Zabronsky for Jack Chew. Michael Low for and with Shirley Zisman.

Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to enforceability of the language (provision) in the Survivor's Trust relating to release of liability, pro rata distributions, delay in administration of the Trust, lack of cooperation between the two Trustees, removal of the co-trustee, etc. Counsel reported that discovery has not been completed and that an evidentiary hearing may need to be scheduled. Counsel is required to submit points and authorities regarding enforceability of said provision which are due by May 10, 2013 at 9:00 a.m. The Petition for Removal of Trustee, etc. and the Petition for Instructions were continued to June 10, 2013 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status on discovery. The Court will provide a ruling on the release issue on that date.


Judge: Lillian K. Sing. Clerk: Nancy Bautista/Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy M. Benton.

Petition For Instructions Pursuant To Probate Code Sec 17200
Off Calendar May-06-2013 Continued To Jun-10-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Instructions Pursuant To Probate Code Sec 17200
Off Calendar Feb-27-2013 Continued To May-06-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Robert Elliott for Schirley Zidman. Michelle Trausch for Mordechai Winter (Respondent). Thomas Latham for Michael McQuiller and Jack Chu (Co-Trustees of the Life Insurance Trust).

Mr. Elliott urged the Court to hear the matter today stating that a lot of time has been spent, that they hired a Court Reporter and that the matter has been in abeyance for one year already. Ms. Trausch stated that she has not had the opportunity to address all of the pleadings she was served with or refute the declaration. Mr. Latham has no position on continuing the matter or not. The Petition for Instructions was continued to May 6, 2013 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for all of the issues to determined and heard at the same time.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Michelle Savage, CSR#12957 (209) 662-0101. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy M. Benton.
